rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Merit Hearing On Petitioner S Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Tuesday, May-12-2026, LINE 10. RESPONDENT STEVEN GORDON's, DIRECTOR FOR THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES FOR THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Joint Notice Of Merit Hearing On Petitioner's Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Per order of the Presiding Judge, the motion is transferred to be heard in Department 302 in front of Judge Quinn on June 5, 2026. Judge Van Aken is recused. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DAVID M. SCHMITT, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 877 And 877.6
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Tuesday, May-12-2026, LINE 1. CROSS DEFENDANT ALCAL SPECIALTY CONTRACTING, INC.'s, A NEVADA CORPORATION, Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 877 And 877.6. The unopposed motion for good-faith settlement determination is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. This determination bars "any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based on comparative negligence or comparative fault." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 877.6, subd. (c).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronin?S Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Arpie A. Aslanian; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff; [Proposed] Order Of The Court
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Tuesday, May-12-2026, LINE 2. DEFENDANT GARY KAPLAN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Kathleen Cronin's Deposition; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff. The unopposed motion to compel the deposition of plaintiff Kathleen Cronin is granted. Cronin shall appear for deposition at a mutually agreed time within 20 days of this order. Cronin's failure to appear or to meet and confer regarding prior deposition notices lacks substantial justification and Cronin shall pay $1091.50 to defendant Gary Kaplan within 30 days. Cronin is cautioned that failure to comply with court orders concerning discovery may result in further monetary sanctions or evidentiary, issue, or terminating sanctions. rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA INSURANCE GUARANTEE ASSOCIATION VS. C&J LEASING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Scriveners Error And Request For Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Tuesday, May-12-2026, LINE 4. PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA INSURANCE GUARANTEE ASSOCIATION's, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO GATEWAY INSURANCE COMPANY, IN LIQUIDATION, Motion For Relief From Scriveners Error And Request For Entry Of Default. The unopposed motion for entry of default is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. The omission of the descriptor "as successor in interest to Gateway Insurance Company, in liquidation" from the summons does not defeat notice where plaintiff served the complaint with the summons. "[T]he provisions of the [Code of Civil Procedure] and all proceedings under it are to be liberally construed, with a view to effect its objects and to promote justice." (Hammer Collections Co. v. Ironsides Computer Corp. (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 899, 902 [citation simplified].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCUS DYLAN ROSARIO VS. SUBHI ABDELHALIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Tuesday, May-12-2026, LINE 8. 3-PLAINTIFF FRANCISCUS ROSARIO's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Franciscus Dylan Rosario's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint (SAC) is denied. Defendants CSAA Insurance Exchange; Michael R. Chambers; and Carbone, Smith, & Koyama filed a special motion to strike Rosario's first amended complaint (FAC) under California's anti-SLAPP statute on April 9, 2025. Rosario subsequently filed the instant motion for leave to file an SAC on April 21, 2026, and it was noticed for hearing before the anti-SLAPP motion was noticed for hearing. Generally, a pending anti-SLAPP motion does not prevent a plaintiff from filing an amended complaint. (Dickinson v. Cosby (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 655, 678.) However, "[a] plaintiff ... may not seek to subvert or avoid a ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the challenged complaint ... in response to the motion." (Contreras v. Dowling (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 774, 791.) Courts generally permit amendment despite a pending anti-SLAPP motion when the amendments would not affect the outcome of the pending motion, such as adding a new defendant. (See, e.g., Dickinson, supra, 17 Cal.App.5th at 680.) Plaintiff's declaration does not attach the proposed SAC. However, it appears from his description that he seeks to add allegations concerning the same litigation conduct that the FAC concerns. Because the court has by companion order granted the anti-SLAPP motion and stricken the FAC, this motion is denied as moot. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
